clips.text: Welcome to Reindustrialize. You are about to launch a manufacturing company of your own, starting with an almost-empty garage and one chance to prove the shop can deliver.
Every manufacturing powerhouse starts with small decisions made well: the right material, a safe setup, a good part, a reliable team, and a promise kept to the customer.
Choose the founder who represents you, give them a name, and name the company you want to build. I will mentor you, but every decision belongs to you.
Welcome to the shop. This is your manufacturing company. You will start small, learn every station, and build it into an industrial powerhouse.
Before we take the first order, learn the floor. Material enters at receiving, moves through preparation and machining, then leaves only after inspection and a clean handoff.
This is the planning desk. Open it to accept customer work, read the print, confirm the material, and understand the route before anyone touches the stock.
The NOX terminal is where you order certified raw material. Match the alloy and size to the job, check the cost, then confirm delivery to receiving.
The bandsaw turns delivered stock into a controlled blank. Open the station, enter the required cut length, and leave enough material for clean finishing operations.
The tool cart is your setup station. Choose the correct cutter, control stickout, gauge the probe and chamfer tool, and install the complete kit before machining.
The vertical machining center makes the part while its timer runs. Confirm the blank and tool, then return to the floor or reopen the station to watch the cut.
Shop Class is where we stop and learn the reason behind the process. Use it to review speeds, feeds, material behavior, quality, and the math that protects the machine.
The manual mill teaches direct control of workholding, spindle speed, feed, and depth. Its production lesson is orientation only until the manual machining task is enabled.
The deburr bench removes sharp edges and prepares the part for inspection. The future task will require the correct edge break without damaging a finished feature.
The CNC lathe rotates the stock against the tool. Inspect chuck pressure, stock stickout, turret clearance, and the program before any production cycle.
The MTConnect node exposes machine status and production signals. It becomes useful when the shop grows from watching one machine to managing an entire connected system.
The JobLine handoff terminal records what ran, what comes next, and what looks unusual. A clean handoff prevents the next shift from starting with missing context.
Receiving is where certified stock enters the shop. Verify the alloy, dimensions, quantity, and certification before material moves into the production route.
The mission board shows the current objective and chapter gate. When you are unsure, follow the pulsing target or use Go To and Open to reach the correct station.
Watch the route from left to right. Confirm material before preparation, complete every required setup before machining, inspect before shipping, and never advance a job with a missing operation.
At planning, open the customer order and read material, dimensions, tolerance, finish, quantity, and due date. Accept the job only when you understand its route and can support the promise.
At the NOX catalog, select the alloy named on the traveler, compare dimensions and certification, verify the coin cost, and confirm the order. Success creates a delivery record for receiving.
At the bandsaw, start with an accepted job and delivered material. Enter the traveler cut length, include finish allowance, keep hands clear, and confirm the blank before it moves forward.
At the tool cart, match the primary cutter to the feature, set only the stickout needed for clearance, then gauge the probe and chamfer tool. The VMC stays locked until all three are ready.

At the VMC, match the blank to the part picture, secure the workholding, and confirm the complete tool kit. Start the timed simulation, watch the cut when you want, and inspect the finished shape before shipping.
Use Shop Class when a result does not make sense. Review the formula or principle, answer the knowledge check, and return to the station understanding why the corrected process should work.
The future manual mill task will require secure workholding, edge finding, spindle selection, controlled feed, measured depth, and a safe stop. For now, use this stop to learn its location and role.
The future deburr task will ask you to identify required edges, choose a safe tool, apply a consistent edge break, protect critical features, and confirm the part is safe to handle before inspection.
Open the lathe inspection view and trace the stock, chuck, turret, tools, tailstock, and control. Production remains locked, but you can already verify clearance and recognize unsafe stickout.
The future connectivity task will map machine states, confirm signals, identify downtime, and compare actual cycle behavior with the plan. For now, verify where the node sits on the data lane.
The future handoff task will record job status, completed operations, next action, quality concerns, and unusual conditions. A passing handoff gives the next operator enough context to act safely.

At receiving, compare the purchase record with the physical stock and certification. Quarantine anything with the wrong alloy, size, quantity, damage, or missing paperwork before it reaches a machine.
Read the mission board from top to bottom: current chapter, completed tasks, job count, quality gate, and next achievable step. Use Go To and Open whenever you need the game to route you correctly.
I'm Zach. I'll teach you how real shops win while you choose the customers, equipment, people, and risks.
Your first customer job is on the planning desk. I advise. You own every decision.
The customer is hiring your company, not me. Read the requirements, decide whether your shop can keep the promise, and release the work order only when you are ready.
The NOX delivery has arrived with the material certification. Verify the alloy, dimensions, quantity, and paperwork before you release this stock to your process.
Everything on this floor obeys math. Let me show you.
Scrap isn't failure. Scrap you don't learn from is failure.
You have outgrown the garage. Now build systems, not just parts.
Start with certified stock. Match the alloy, thickness, and quantity to the print before you spend a dollar.
Cut the raw stock to length before it goes near the CNC. Leave enough material to face both ends cleanly.
Set the right tools, confirm each offset, and keep the stickout as short as the job allows.
Read the program one block at a time. Know where the tool will go before you press cycle start.
The first part is evidence, not inventory. Inspect it before you commit the rest of the material.
Good part. Record what worked so the next operator can repeat it.
Stop and find the cause. Fix the process before another piece becomes scrap.
Hire for the work you need now and the capability you will need next. A strong team compounds.
You made the hiring decision. Now give your new teammate a clear station assignment, a safe standard, and enough context to succeed in your company.
Your company has shipped five customer jobs at a passing quality average. The garage proved your process, your judgment, and your promise to customers.
Cash keeps the doors open. A new machine only helps when the work, people, and payment plan support it.
This job shop gives you more capacity and more ways to fail. Standardize the handoffs before volume exposes them.
You built more than a bigger building. You built a manufacturing system that can keep its promises.
We are here to help reindustrialize and help manufacturing dominate in America.
Start with the print. Find the material, feature, tolerance, and finish before you touch the setup.
Slow it down. One decision at a time: what do you know, what do you need, and what can hurt the part?
If the setup does not look right, stop. A safe pause costs less than a crash.
That one missed. Reset the step, use what the result taught you, and try it again.
Good correction. You changed the process instead of hoping for a different result.
Do not assume it worked. Verify the result, record it, and make the next step repeatable.
The why matters because the next job will look different. Learn the principle, not just this answer.
Leave the next person a clean handoff: what ran, what comes next, and what looks unusual.
Protect the cash. Revenue sounds good, but margin and payment timing keep the shop alive.
Good question. Strong machinists ask before uncertainty becomes scrap.
Nice work. Now write down what made it work so the whole team can repeat it.
You have the next step. Do it carefully, confirm the result, then come back if something does not match.
I started in a garage, named my own company, and Zach helped me turn one small job into a real manufacturing business.
I actually cut the stock, set the CNC tools, checked the G code, and inspected my first part. Every step feels connected.
What hooked me was outgrowing the garage, hiring a team, and choosing bigger equipment until my shop became a manufacturing powerhouse.

[confident] American manufacturing is moving again. [building excitement] In REINDUSTRIALIZE, you do not watch from the sidelines. You build the company. Start in an empty garage. Choose your founder. Learn from Zach. Win the work, order certified material, cut stock, set tools, read real G and M code, and inspect the first article. Then hire the team, expand the floor, and turn one reliable job into a manufacturing powerhouse. [inspiring] This is where builders learn how production really works—one decision, one part, and one promise kept at a time. Your first shift starts now.

tracks.prompt: Original instrumental game soundtrack for a hopeful American manufacturing startup at dawn. Warm analog synthesizer, restrained electric guitar, metallic percussion and a steady entrepreneurial pulse. Seamless-feeling intro and outro, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

Original instrumental loop-friendly background music for a small working machine shop. Mid-tempo industrial groove, muted drums, bass, subtle CNC-inspired rhythmic clicks, focused and optimistic, never distracting, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

Original instrumental game music for a precision CNC challenge. Controlled tension, pulsing electronic rhythm, clean mechanical accents, increasing momentum without aggression, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

Original triumphant instrumental game soundtrack for moving from a garage into a large manufacturing facility. Broad drums, warm synth brass, industrial percussion, confident forward motion, earned rather than bombastic, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

Original understated instrumental strategy-game background cue for reviewing manufacturing jobs, cash flow and hiring decisions. Warm electric piano, quiet analog pulse, light brushed percussion, analytical but optimistic, loop-friendly, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

Original sparse instrumental game cue for dimensional inspection and first-article approval. Precise mallet tones, soft low synth, restrained ticking rhythm, calm concentration with a clean resolution, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

Original instrumental endgame manufacturing-management soundtrack for a vast modern American factory campus. Expansive analog synths, confident drums, subtle orchestral weight and rhythmic industrial textures, strategic and forward-looking, no vocals, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation.

effects.prompt: Seamlessly looping ambience inside a small clean machine shop: distant ventilation, subdued electric motor hum, occasional faint metal handling, no voices, no music, no alarms, controlled safe workplace.
Seamlessly looping ambience inside a large modern manufacturing facility: broad HVAC, distant CNC machinery, forklifts far away, subtle room reflections, no voices, no music, no alarms.

Seamlessly looping restrained ambience in a tidy small job shop: soft HVAC, one distant enclosed CNC at low level, occasional very faint metal handling and room reflections, calm and focused, no close machinery, voices, music, alarms, beeps or forklift horns.

Seamlessly looping quiet planning-office ambience beside a machine shop: low ventilation, very distant muffled production through a closed wall, occasional subtle paper movement, calm and unobtrusive, no voices, phone ringing, keyboard, music, alarms or tonal hum.

Seamlessly looping calm night ambience inside a clean automated manufacturing facility: low HVAC, sparse distant enclosed machine activity, subtle electrical room tone and large-room reflections, controlled and reassuring, no voices, music, alarms, beeps or sudden events.

Industrial CNC machine main power switching on: firm rotary disconnect click, contactor clunk, electronics wake, cooling fans rise to a steady idle, isolated realistic shop sound, no voice, music, alarm or startup melody.
Industrial CNC machine controlled power down: spindle already stopped, cooling fans wind down, contactor releases with a firm click, isolated realistic shop sound, no voice, music or alarm.
Seamlessly looping powered-on CNC machining center at idle: enclosure ventilation, servo electronics and faint coolant pump room tone, spindle stopped, no cutting, no voices, music or alarms.

Modern CNC milling spindle starts from zero and smoothly accelerates to medium-high speed inside a closed enclosure, healthy bearings, isolated realistic sound, no cutting, chatter, voice, music or alarm.
Seamlessly looping modern CNC milling spindle running steadily at medium-high speed inside a closed enclosure, healthy smooth motor and bearings, no cutting, chatter, voice, music or alarm.
Modern CNC milling spindle smoothly decelerates from medium-high speed to a complete controlled stop inside a closed enclosure, isolated realistic sound, no cutting, crash, voice, music or alarm.
CNC flood coolant pump engages and coolant begins striking a metal workpiece inside an enclosure, isolated realistic transition, no spindle, cutting, voice, music or alarm.
CNC flood coolant pump switches off and the remaining liquid quickly drains and drips inside the enclosure, isolated realistic transition, no spindle, cutting, voice, music or alarm.

Seamlessly looping four-flute carbide end mill making a stable side-and-pocket cut in 6061 aluminum inside a closed CNC enclosure: crisp regular flute engagement, smooth spindle, coolant flow, healthy chips, no chatter, crash, voice, music or alarm.

Seamlessly looping carbide twist drill performing controlled peck drilling in 6061 aluminum inside a closed CNC enclosure: spindle, repeated feed engagement and retract, light coolant, clean healthy cut, no chatter, crash, voice, music or alarm.

Seamlessly looping ball nose end mill contouring 6061 aluminum inside a closed CNC enclosure: smooth lighter flute engagement that subtly changes with surface contact, coolant flow, healthy cut, no chatter, crash, voice, music or alarm.
Industrial horizontal bandsaw motor switches on and blade accelerates to stable speed before touching material, isolated realistic shop sound, no cutting, voice, music or alarm.
Industrial horizontal bandsaw blade motor switches off and coasts smoothly to a stop, isolated realistic shop sound, no cutting, voice, music or alarm.

Seamlessly looping manual vertical milling machine making a light controlled cut in aluminum: steady spindle, hand-fed table, crisp healthy cutter engagement, no chatter, crash, voice, music or alarm.
Seamlessly looping careful hand deburring of a machined aluminum edge with a swivel deburring tool and fine file, close realistic metal scraping, controlled and gentle, no grinder, voice or music.
CNC touch probe approaches a metal workpiece, makes a delicate precise contact click, then retracts, isolated close realistic sound, no spindle, voice, music or alarm.
Short controlled compressed-air blow clearing aluminum chips from a machined part inside a shop, isolated realistic sound, no voice, music or alarm.

Seamlessly looping CNC machining center G00 rapid traverse with spindle running but tool clear of material: fast controlled servo-axis motion inside an enclosure, no cutting engagement, crash, chatter, voice, music or alarm.
Heavy CNC machining center safety door unlatches and slides fully open, isolated realistic shop sound, no alarm, voice or music.
Machinist tightens a precision milling vise on aluminum stock with a firm wrench pull and restrained metal clamp sound, isolated realistic foley, no voice or music.
CNC toolholder taper is inserted cleanly into a spindle and clamps with a precise mechanical drawbar snap, isolated realistic shop sound, no alarm, voice or music.
Heavy organized machinist tool-cart drawer slides open on ball-bearing rails and stops softly, isolated realistic shop foley, no loose tool crash, voice or music.
Precision CNC collet nut reaches specified torque with one firm torque-wrench click, isolated close realistic metal foley, no voice or music.
Horizontal bandsaw vise closes and firmly clamps aluminum bar stock, controlled screw and metal contact, isolated realistic shop sound, no motor, voice or music.
Manufacturing job traveler folder opens, two paper sheets turn, and a firm approval stamp lands, close isolated office-shop foley, no voice or music.
Industrial shop-floor terminal wakes with a quiet relay click and restrained original electronic confirmation, concise, no branded startup tone, voice or music.
Original restrained industrial terminal confirmation sound: clean tactile button click and short neutral electronic acknowledgement, no voice, melody or music.
Shop instructor writes a short formula on a chalkboard with two clear chalk strokes, isolated classroom-shop foley, no voice or music.
CNC lathe hydraulic three-jaw chuck closes and firmly clamps round metal stock, isolated realistic machine-shop sound, spindle stopped, no alarm, voice or music.
CNC lathe turret unlocks, indexes one station, and locks precisely, isolated realistic mechanical sound, no cutting, alarm, voice or music.
Industrial network cable clicks into a machine data node followed by a restrained original connection confirmation, isolated, no modem sound, voice or music.
Operator badge scan, short keyboard confirmation, and restrained shift-handoff sent tone, isolated industrial terminal foley, no voice or music.
Magnetic task card snaps onto a metal production board followed by a concise original objective-update ping, isolated, no voice or music.
Seamlessly looping autonomous mobile robot driving slowly on a smooth factory floor: quiet electric traction motor, soft wheel noise and one restrained proximity cue, no voice, music or alarm.
Seamlessly looping collaborative robot making a slow safe pick-and-place motion: clean electric servo movement and gentle gripper actuation, no impact, alarm, voice or music.
CNC toolholder passes an RFID tool-crib reader followed by a restrained original identification confirmation, isolated realistic shop sound, no voice or music.
A rugged corded office phone rings twice in a small machine-shop office, clear but not harsh, isolated realistic sound, no voice or music.
Rugged corded office phone handset lifts from its cradle with a firm plastic click and subtle line connection, isolated realistic foley, no voice or music.
Industrial horizontal bandsaw cutting solid aluminum bar stock, steady blade motor, coolant trickle, clean controlled cut, isolated sound effect, no voices, no music.
Heavy CNC machining center safety door sliding shut and latching firmly, isolated shop sound, no alarm, no voice.
Seamlessly looping enclosed CNC milling cycle: spindle at moderate speed, controlled aluminum cutting, coolant flow, stable healthy machine, no chatter, crash, alarm, voice or music.
Modern CNC automatic tool change inside an enclosure: spindle orient, mechanical carousel index, crisp tool clamp, isolated sound, no alarm or crash.
Seamlessly looping enclosed CNC lathe cutting steel: stable spindle, controlled tool engagement, light coolant, healthy machine, no chatter, crash, alarm, voice or music.
Digital caliper jaws slide and gently click against a machined metal part, close detailed isolated foley, no voice.
Precision micrometer ratchet makes three delicate clicks while measuring a metal part, close isolated foley, no voice.
Forklift lowers a pallet of metal stock onto a concrete factory floor, hydraulic release and solid restrained pallet thud, no reversing alarm, no voice.
Original positive industrial game quest-complete stinger: three bright metallic notes, subtle pneumatic accent, confident and concise, no voice, no recognizable melody.
Original clean quality-inspection success UI sound: precise bright metal ping with a soft electronic confirmation, concise, no voice.
Original restrained quality-inspection warning UI sound: low muted metal tone and short neutral electronic alert, informative not punishing, no voice.
Original compact manufacturing game reward sound: two small machined metal tokens clink with a soft upward electronic sparkle, no voice.
Original industrial facility-unlock game stinger: rolling shop-door mechanism, deep confident impact and hopeful tonal rise, no voice, no recognizable melody.
